source: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 — Gazette of India, 11 Aug 2023

clauses.title: Grounds for processing
clauses.obligation: Process personal data only for a lawful purpose with consent or for legitimate uses.
clauses.immediate_action: Document lawful basis for each processing activity; maintain RoPA.
clauses.liability_note: Processing without a valid ground is an independent contravention.

clauses.title: Notice to Data Principal
clauses.obligation: Provide plain-language notice at or before request for consent.
clauses.immediate_action: Publish vendor-specific privacy notice in English + 22 scheduled languages.
clauses.liability_note: Opaque notices have been a leading cause of DPB observations.

clauses.title: Consent
clauses.obligation: Valid, informed, free and unambiguous consent before processing personal data.
clauses.immediate_action: Pause data collection; publish consent notice; collect fresh consents within 30 days.
clauses.liability_note: Data fiduciary (you) is liable, not the vendor, under §6 read with §8.

clauses.title: Legitimate uses
clauses.obligation: Only the enumerated legitimate-use cases may bypass consent (employment, emergency, etc.).
clauses.immediate_action: Map each vendor share to a specific sub-clause of §7 or discontinue.
clauses.liability_note: Misuse of the legitimate-use exemption is heavily scrutinised by DPB.

clauses.title: Purpose limitation via contract
clauses.obligation: Engage data processors only under a valid contract that limits processing to stated purposes.
clauses.immediate_action: Audit vendor DPA; add purpose-limitation and sub-processor clauses.
clauses.liability_note: No contract = direct §8(4) violation even if no breach occurred.

clauses.title: Reasonable security safeguards
clauses.obligation: Implement reasonable security safeguards to prevent personal data breach.
clauses.immediate_action: Isolate vendor access; rotate credentials; preserve logs for DPB.
clauses.liability_note: Failure of vendor safeguard = failure of data fiduciary under §8(5).

clauses.title: Breach notification
clauses.obligation: Notify the Data Protection Board and affected data principals of a personal data breach.
clauses.immediate_action: File intimation with DPB within 72 hours; notify affected users.
clauses.liability_note: Delay in notification is itself a separate penalisable offence.

clauses.title: Data minimisation and erasure
clauses.obligation: Erase personal data upon withdrawal of consent or when purpose is served.
clauses.immediate_action: Audit retention; purge stale records; update retention SLA with vendor.
clauses.liability_note: Retention beyond stated purpose is an independent breach.

clauses.title: Grievance redressal
clauses.obligation: Publish an accessible grievance mechanism with defined response times.
clauses.immediate_action: Publish DPO email; set SLA of 7 working days for first response.
clauses.liability_note: Missing grievance channel is among the most commonly cited violations.

clauses.title: Processing of children's data
clauses.obligation: Verifiable parental consent before processing children's personal data; no tracking/targeted ads.
clauses.immediate_action: Gate children endpoints; age-verify; remove behavioural tracking.

clauses.title: Significant Data Fiduciary obligations
clauses.obligation: Appoint DPO, conduct DPIA, audit data fiduciary duties.
clauses.immediate_action: Trigger DPIA; appoint DPO if classified as SDF.
clauses.liability_note: Applicable if DPB classifies company as SDF based on volume/sensitivity.

clauses.title: Right to access / correction / erasure
clauses.obligation: Provide data principals access, correction, completion, updating and erasure.
clauses.immediate_action: Build DSR workflow; ensure vendor can respond within 14 days.
clauses.liability_note: Vendors that cannot return data on request create cascading §11 liability.

clauses.title: Cross-border transfer
clauses.obligation: Transfer personal data outside India only to jurisdictions notified by the Central Government.
clauses.immediate_action: Review vendor data residency; add data-localisation clause if needed.
clauses.liability_note: Unlisted-jurisdiction transfers invite both DPDP and sector-regulator action.

clauses.title: Exemptions
clauses.obligation: Narrowly scoped exemptions — research, statistical, state-security.
clauses.immediate_action: Do not over-claim exemption without DPO sign-off.
clauses.liability_note: Over-broad exemption claims are routinely rejected by DPB.

clauses.title: Maximum financial penalties
clauses.obligation: Schedule of penalties for each of the above obligations.
clauses.immediate_action: Prepare mitigation plan to argue lesser penalty before DPB.
clauses.liability_note: Board may consider nature, gravity, duration, repetitiveness and gain.
